summary: Soundboard partial with rustic but listenable sound captures a historic early‑psychedelic jam featuring the rare Caution > Feedback sequence and Mickey Hart’s first sit‑in.
review: This fragment showcases an early Dead experiment – a swirling Caution into Feedback jam that many reviewers describe as an "acid freakout" moment. The tape has a noticeable splice at the start and a slightly rustic fidelity, but it remains quite listenable. Highlights include Mickey Hart’s debut on drums and the historic vibe noted in Phil Lesh’s recollections.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers praise the performance and its historical significance, while noting only modest recording imperfections; weighted rating reflects strong show quality and decent soundboard audio.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Tape splice audible at the start; overall rustic but clear enough for listening; occasional mild mud‑ness noted but generally listenable.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Fragmentary recording focuses on an early psychedelic jam; the Caution > Feedback sequence provides a vivid, acid‑laden snapshot of the band's evolution in late 1967.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Mentioned for warmly welcoming Mickey Hart after the show, calling it "THIS is the Grateful Dead".
band_member_comments.Phil: Recalled the gig in his book, noting its experimental nature and Mickey’s first appearance.
band_member_comments.Drums: Mickey Hart sits in on drums toward the end – possibly his first performance with the band.
